_description: Per-question rule tier and rationale for Spain vs Belgium QF (2026-07-10). No crowd/market data used this session by explicit instruction -- RULE1's usual 'avg(crowd, sharp market)' anchor does not apply anywhere in this match; every question falls back to RULE3 (tier2_realdata top priority) or model-only reasoning.

questions.text: Will Spain advance to the semifinals?
questions.rationale: No market/crowd. 3 independent methods (ordered logit, Dixon-Coles grid, Monte Carlo sim) converge tightly (0.777-0.818, blended 0.799). Applied a small humility discount for going in without any market cross-check this match, not a gut override of the research.

questions.text: Will the match be tied at halftime?
questions.rationale: Monte Carlo path simulation output trusted directly (0.4109).

questions.text: Will Belgium hold a lead at any point in regulation?
questions.rationale: Path-dependent question, not answerable from a static goal model -- Monte Carlo simulation is the right tool. Trusted directly (0.3163).

questions.text: Will either team make a substitution at halftime?
questions.rationale: New base rate this session (data/processed/halftime_sub_panel.csv). Small-sample blend: overall corpus 61.0% (n=59) vs knockout-only 50.0% (n=10) -- blended toward the thinner but more contextually relevant knockout figure per RULE5, not fully committed to either.

questions.text: Will a goal be scored before the first hydration break?
questions.rationale: Early-window timing questions are a project-validated-safe pattern (KNOCKOUT_STAGE_PRICING_DEEP_DIVE.md 3.4: all 3 early-window instances positive, +30.86 combined). Simulation output trusted without extra shading (0.4622).

questions.text: Will a goal be scored after the second hydration break but before the end of regulation?
questions.rationale: Late-window timing questions are a project-flagged soft spot (deep dive 4.1: 3 of 3 late-window instances negative, -45.90 combined). Simulation output (0.4595) is already close to 0.5, so the required 'shade toward 0.5' discipline needs little additional adjustment -- nudged slightly toward 0.50 anyway per the documented lesson.

questions.text: Will both teams score in regulation?
questions.rationale: Dixon-Coles grid (0.4310) and MC sim (0.4259) agree closely -- averaged.

questions.text: Will Lamine Yamal score or assist a goal?
questions.rationale: Same underlying pattern validated in Portugal-Spain (priced 0.30, settled NO, +38.48 RBP) -- this would be the 3rd consecutive application on the same player. Kept close to his real in-tournament rate (~25-27%) rather than the higher figure a name/reputation-driven read would suggest, but not pushed lower just because it worked before -- that would be chasing the pattern past what the data supports.

questions.text: Will Kevin De Bruyne have 1+ SOT?
questions.rationale: Two-stage estimate: P(features tonight) x P(1+SOT | features). Participation is a genuine open uncertainty (0 minutes in R16, first such occurrence all tournament) -- judged at 0.82 given QF stakes make a second straight rest less likely, not a data-derived figure. Conditional SOT rate is an EB shrink of the small 2026 sample (0.75, n=4) toward the larger historical StatsBomb sample (0.40, n=10).

Participation near-certain (0.92 -- ever-present impact sub in all 5 games). Conditional SOT rate correctly uses the full 5-game 1+SOT rate (3/5=0.60), not the 3-game goal-scoring streak (which is a shots-to-goals conversion streak, a different and much smaller-sample claim) -- EB-shrunk toward the historical 0.40 rate (n=10).

EB-shrunk Poisson (0.6249) used over the raw 2026-only figure (0.7763/0.75 empirical) specifically because Belgium is a materially stronger opponent than 3 of Spain's 4 prior games (Cape Verde, Saudi Arabia, Austria) -- closer in quality to Uruguay, the one game where Spain's SOT dropped to 1. Regime-coverage lesson from the Diogo Costa call in Portugal-Spain applied here in the suppressing direction.
Very strong signal (5/5 empirical, 0.9116 shrunk-Poisson) capped per RULE6's ceiling discipline (never submit near-100%) rather than pushed to the raw model output.

Perfect 4/4 empirical record, but small n and the identical question type in the last match (vs a different opponent, Portugal) only barely cleared the threshold in practice (final corners just above 6 in a close call). Priced above last match's 0.54 given the stronger signal here, but not at the raw shrunk-Poisson ceiling (0.6547).

Blended three signals with explicit weights: team-level own-discipline history (40%, both teams have been clean so far), Michael Oliver's personal card rate (35%, elevated but pulled up by one outlier match), corpus-wide baseline (25%). This is the first match this project has priced with a referee-specific card signal.

questions.text: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown?
questions.rationale: Priced the two components separately then combined assuming quasi-independence: penalty rate shrunk from Oliver's thin 1/3 sample toward a 0.25 general prior; red-card rate shrunk from Oliver's 0/3 (no ejections shown all tournament, despite the high yellow count) toward a 0.13 general prior.
